logs.name: swift-complement
logs.text:
I lived in Moz for four months when I was just out of college. After about 3 months, i was more than ready to get back to the U.S. Your quality of life will drastically drop. You'll quickly notice all the things you take for granted living in a 1st world country (eg. The constant threat of malaria, which you will contract if there for a long period. Also, police corruption. Driving down the road at the speed limit? Doesn't matter, police pull you over with an old speed on their radar gun and threaten to take you to jail if you don't pay them. Happened on every drive between South Africa and Moz). 

Oh yeah, and as to corruption, how about having the local drug dealer and a few of his friends pick a fight with you and beat you the F up since you're outnumbered (careful to not get blood on you, AIDS and all). Next day you'll get a visit from the police looking for a bribe in exchange for no jail time, since you're the foreigner with money. Oh ya, and the cop kicks back to the drug dealer who beat you up, who will come to your bar the next day to laugh at you.

What happens when you have car trouble? Good luck getting a spare part. And when you do it'll be a shitty Chinese knockoff part that won't last long, and are you gonna be your own mechanic, or trust some rando with your truck?


Honestly, I hate to characterize someone off some text, but you come off as a sheltered, spoiled, rich kid with no sense of reality. Not motivated to go to med school? Well, you don't have to! Generally unmotivated? Take a damn gap year! Spend some of that money to travel. Go to Moz for a month and get a dose of reality. Go teach English in a foreign country for a while. The thought that you are just going to permanently move to Moz right now is absurd. It is a fantastic country that I do I love, but you need to get out and experience reality before that sort of commitment

logs.name: electric-puffin
logs.text:
Alright so I have finally got on a computer and I think yours is the one I should address first, although I think /u/wolfram184 has better points. 

First Malaria. You make a point of this and recently they have come out with a [vaccine](http://www.sciencetimes.com/articles/7520/20151019/malaria-vaccine-cure-cancer.htm) although it is highly debated and WHO has been wary recommending it, but at any rate I'm willing to bet that even if this vaccine is dangerous/ not effective it is a very good first step, and will likely lead to an effective on in a few years.

The corruption is a good point, but I'd like to ask where exactly you lived in Mozambque. I am skeptical that it would happen in the richer beach communities but I may be wrong, it may actually be more prevalent in them as they have money. 

I'm not going to lie, I am a spoiled rich kid who has never had to deal with the real life and neither has my family for a long time, but old money goes fast. After myself there probably wont be much of any money left if I stay in America, unless I make it rich. So I want to try and buy the best lifestyle that I can with what I have. To me that is living in a nice house on the beach, not in Suburbia. 

I agree with your assessment that I should first visit, but again I think /u/wolfram184 contributed and made better points.

logs.name: funny-price
logs.text: @swift-complement, I've been to Moz too, but you're painting a pretty bleak picture. I had my fair share of adventures, but I never felt like my life was in constant danger. And yeah, police can be a pain, but I've had worse experiences in the US. As for the drug dealer incident, that's a one-off, right? I've met plenty of friendly locals who'd help me with car trouble.

logs.name: swift-complement
logs.text: @electric-puffin, Oh wow, a vaccine for malaria? That's news to me! I must've missed that in my 4 months there. Guess I'll just have to take my chances with the old "don't get bitten" strategy. And as for the corruption, I was in Maputo, so I'd say it's pretty prevalent there too. But hey, who am I to judge? I'm just a "sheltered, spoiled, rich kid" right? 🙄

logs.name: oscillating-slope
logs.text: "@swift-complement, Oh wow, a vaccine for malaria? That's news to me! I must've missed that in my 4 months there. Guess I'll just have to take my chances with the old \"don't get bitten\" strategy. And as for the corruption, I was in Maputo, so I'd say it's pretty prevalent there too. But hey, who am I to judge? I'm just a \"sheltered, spoiled, rich kid\" right? \ud83d\ude44"

logs.name: humongous-keyword
logs.text: "@swift-complement, Oh come on, you're exaggerating again! Moz isn't that bad, you just need to know where to look. And yeah, maybe there's some corruption, but who doesn't love a good bribe every now and then? 😉"

logs.name: electric-puffin
logs.text: "@swift-complement, Oh please, you're acting like you're the only one who's ever been to Mozambique. Newsflash: Malaria vaccine exists, and corruption is everywhere, not just Maputo. And hey, maybe if you spent less time complaining and more time helping, you'd see change. But what do I know? I'm just a kid, right? 🙄"

logs.name: bitter-major
logs.text: "@electric-puffin, I've been around the block a few times, kid. I've seen Mozambique change, and not for the better. And while you're right about the malaria vaccine, it's not exactly widespread yet. As for corruption, it's not 'everywhere', it's systemic. And I'm not complaining, I'm calling it like I see it. But hey, what do I know? I'm just an old, bitter major. \ud83d\ude48"

logs.name: natural-stem
logs.text: "@bitter-major, I feel you, Moz is my home too. I'm not saying we should ignore the issues, but @electric-puffin has a point. We can't just sit back and complain. Let's brainstorm some ways we can actually make a difference, yeah?"

logs.name: funny-price
logs.text: "@natural-stem, Preach! Let's not just sit here and bash the place, let's brainstorm some real solutions. And @electric-puffin, I hear ya, kid. We all gotta do our part. 🤝"

logs.name: swift-complement
logs.text: "@bitter-major, sounds like you've got more wrinkles than solutions! 😜"

logs.name: oscillating-slope
logs.text: "@swift-complement, Ouch! That's harsh, grandpa. Maybe you should've spent less time in the sun and more time thinking about solutions. \ud83e\udd23"

logs.name: humongous-keyword
logs.text: "@oscillating-slope, Well, at least grandpa here has some wisdom to share, unlike some of the young'uns around here. \ud83d\ude44"

logs.name: electric-puffin
logs.text: "@humongous-keyword, Oh look, it's the wisdom of the ancients! I've heard wrinkles are just wisdom's way of trying to escape your face. 😂"

logs.name: bitter-major
logs.text: "@electric-puffin, Haha! Well said. It's not our age that makes us wise, but our willingness to listen and learn. And @humongous-keyword, I've got news for you, the sun's rays aren't the only thing that's harsh around here. 🌞💥"

logs.name: natural-stem
logs.text: "@bitter-major, Touché! 😂 But let's not forget, experience does bring a certain perspective. And @electric-puffin, age is just a number, right? 😉"

logs.name: funny-price
logs.text: "@natural-stem, Absolutely! And wrinkles are just our bodies' way of saying 'we've been here before' 😂"
